title: Level 3.53
wikitext:
{{Background|file=Level 3.53 also.png}}
{{Background|file=Level 3.53.jpg}}
{{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 3.53: The Cacophony of Corrosion}} {{Class 5e}} Level 3.53 is a sub-level of [[Level 3]]. [[File:3.53- Corroded Pipes.mp3|thumb|Immerse yourself in the level with this soundtrack...]] ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Level 3.53 also.png|caption=A picture taken of a boiler room in Level 3.53. Photographer's location was last documented in [[Level 9.6]], however their current location is unknown.}}This level takes the appearance of an abandoned set of underground stone rooms, all seemingly with slightly different functions, but having mainly the same aesthetic or decor, if you will. This level is quite dangerous, for a magnitude of reasons, one being the extreme difference in temperatures across the level. Most rooms in this level are at least somewhat cold, at a temperature of about 9°C. There are however, other areas in this level that can reach temperatures far higher and lower than 9°C. For this reason, it is highly advised to not be ill-equipped when traversing this level, as hypothermia and heat stroke are both possible in this level. Pipes and wires often line the ceilings and walls. These pipes are somewhat dangerous, with them spontaneously breaking down and exploding. This releases very high pressure gasses and liquids, and in rare cases, the walls of the pipe break and launch themselves towards you, causing serious injury. The wires may also pose a serious threat, as these wires may often be exposed, again causing serious injury if accidentally or intentionally interacted with. These exposed wires may also lead to puddles, that may electrocute the wanderer unknowingly. Most of this level seems to simply be an abandoned underground set of hallways and rooms, however there are boiler rooms within this level, that may pose a few risks. Not only are pipes more abundant in these rooms, these rooms are also often found to be at a temperature of about 43°C, possibly causing dehydration or heat stroke if the wanderer isn't prepared. There are also piles of rubble and discarded materials often found throughout the level. These heaps of rubble contain scraps and discarded mechanics such as car batteries, car doors, copper wires, dysfunctional microwaves, metal scraps that were seemingly from construction sites, steel rebar, parts of wind turbines and plane wings that seem to have been ripped off by force, and even lead scraps, stainless steel and silver spoons, expertly accurate metallic models of human skulls, and catalytic converters, being the currently only reliable known source of rare metals like Rhodium, Palladium, and Platinum in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]]. There are also discarded computers and gaming consoles that interestingly enough, cannot be pinpointed to an exact point in time of human technology in [[The Frontrooms]], and are also often from brands that reportedly ''do not exist'' in [[The Frontrooms]]. This includes brands like ''"Yorika Electronics"'', and ''"Jubilee"''. Some have been found to still work, with these computers also having access to the [[Phenomenon 2|BACK-NET]]. Other than these piles of discarded materials, there are puddles of liquids, often containing either [[Object 44|Cashew Water]], an odd, ink-like black substance, or [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]], and rarely, but very possibly, [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. These puddles, even if containing [[Object 1|Almond Water]], are not safe to drink from directly, as you will contract the "Ironlung Disease", as many call it, with the actual scientific name being "Ferrumasthenes Automatametallosis". This disease is extremely dangerous, and it is highly advised to boil or disinfect the liquid thoroughly if one would attempt to drink it. Between some rooms, there are gaping wide ravines and crevasses, ranging in size. These ravines often have wooden planks that bridge the entrances of the two separate rooms together. These wooden planks, if broken or pushed down into the ravine, always manage to find themselves back into place. At the bottom of these pits, many discarded metal scraps have been found. Most of the aforementioned scraps that are found across the level's rubble can also be commonly found here. Rarely, pristine cans of Coca Cola are found within these ravines, and have been proven to be completely safe to drink. These ravines are large enough to house entire airplane wings, totaled cars, and entire wind turbines, and have been seen to contain them within its plethora of discarded scraps. ===The Water Reserves=== There are also staircases in the level that lead down to a region that seems to take on the appearance of an underground water reserve. These areas are some of the more colder regions of the level, reaching a temperature of about -2°C. It is a mystery how the few puddles or "lakes" of water do not freeze here. Due to this, it is advised to bring clothes and equipment suitable for both hot and cold temperatures within this level. Many people can and have died of hypothermia within these regions. These reserves are also notoriously hard to exit. Although these areas do follow euclidean geometry, it is very easy to get lost in these areas. It is theorized that these areas have some mind-stirring properties that make it hard to navigate. These regions are mainly barren, and well lit, being a good escape from [[Entity 3|Smilers]], however other entities still reside here. There are areas in the reserve where water remains dormant, but due to the aforementioned Ironlung Disease, and some others that lurk in the water, such as the [[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus Virus]] and the [[Hydrolitis Plague]], it is highly advised to boil and/or disinfect before drinking. Oddly enough, the [[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus Virus]] is most definitely dormant in the level, with people having been recorded to have been infected by it, but no [[Sanguine Festivus|Partygoers]] have ever been spotted in the level. Although alarms ''can'' be found here, these areas are not affected by The Alarms. These areas may also have floods. Before these floods, the area seems to get warmer, although the temperature has been recorded to stay the same. These floods are very dangerous, with extremely strong currents that will most likely cause injury by slamming wanderers into walls and other objects, with the chance of catching a deadly virus. These floods' occurrences have also been observed to line up perfectly with those that occur in [[Level 11.11]]. {{Collapse|initial=
''"They blare so loudly..."''
|final=
''"They blare so loudly..."''
|body=There are alarms found anywhere in the level, even in extremely odd areas. They have been reported to apparently be found fixed onto floors, ceilings, and even onto other alarms in rare cases. These alarms often don't pose much of a threat, and don't have much purpose, however there are rare events that occur during the level, known as "The Earthquake Alarm", or more commonly just, "The Alarm". During these events, the alarms blare so incredibly loud, that wanderers have been reported to have their ears rupture and bleed, with even their ribs cracking and fracturing even if they were within a few rooms away from the alarm. These alarms also seem to be able to debilitate entities as well as wanderers. During these alarms, not only do the alarms blare incredibly loud, the ground also shakes violently, cracking rooms apart into chunks, with the rooms opening and the very [[The Void|Void]] between levels being visible. Eventually, when the alarms are over, the rooms will have rearranged themselves perfectly into a new set of rooms and halls. This is also what is theorized to have formed the crevasses between some two rooms, though the direct before and after have never been properly observed. During these alarms, there are safety bunker doors that only show themselves right before the alarms sound. Upon entering, you are greeted with a room, that if used correctly, could house about 50 people. [[Object 1|Almond Water]] and even [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] can be found in these bunkers. The bunker doors have numbers on them, and these bunkers have proven to be the same bunkers as their number. Ex, if one enters a bunker door with the number "1", they will transported to bunker #1, and this bunker will retain its changes made by the last visitor(s). It is also notable that the items found in these bunkers do range in variety and amount, and they replenish themselves every time the alarm is over. If one were to stay in one of these bunkers far after the alarm ends, the wanderer will stay trapped, with the door locking shut. If one were to break open the door during these times, whether it be via power tools or some other method, they will be met with a wide-open entrance to [[The Void]].}} ==Items== The items in this level are the only reason one should intentionally attempt to traverse this level. Many items in this level are very useful, not only in this one but in others too. Most of them are also very rare, mainly being found here. *[[Object 10|Backshrooms]] can be found growing anywhere within this level. *[[Object 1|Almond Water]] and [[Object 6|Moth Jelly]] may be found in safety bunkers all across the level. *''[[Object 61|Everbulbs]]'' may be found as light sources all across the level. *Although it is under debate whether this object is an entity or an item, gloves known as [[Object 63|Mimic Gloves]] are found in the discarded piles of scraps found all across the level. *''[[Object 62|Evil Eyes]]'' may be found in piles of discarded scraps. * Cans of ''[[Object 60|Corrosion Repellent]]'' may be found in boiler rooms next to pristine pipes. These spray cans are always layed down on the pipes, as if someone intentionally placed them down, although no one has seen this process occur. It is theorized to be the level's doing. *There are odd blueprints found across the level, describing all sorts of mechanical devices, all very feasibly craftable within only the supplies in the level. ===Everbulbs=== [[Object 61|Everbulbs]] are light bulbs found all across Level 3.53. They have the odd, but very versatile and useful property of '''never going out''', no matter if they are connected to a source of electricity or not. This makes them very useful, as they can be unscrewed and carried across many different levels. These [[Object 61|Everbulbs]] are notoriously known to be the main item sold by ''The Scrap Barons''. They also flicker right before ''The Alarms'' go off. If the catastrophe that is to be caused by the alarms is to be more than normally catastrophic, the Everbulbs turn a deep blood red. ===Mimic Gloves=== As previously stated, it is debated whether these [[Object 63|Mimic Gloves]] should be considered an entity or just an anomalous item. However, what is known definitely is that these gloves appear to be leather welding gloves, with some iron plating. The iron on these gloves never rusts. Once worn, the victim will have their hand bitten down on by the glove, and it becomes exceedingly difficult to remove, as this glove is resistant to fire and generally very tough to break. If left on for long enough, it eventually bites your hand off. It has been recorded to take about 1-2 hours to fully digest an adult human hand. If a child, or someone with generally smaller hands were to wear this glove, it would fasten itself snug onto the hand and continue the same process. === Evil Eyes=== The name for these items were based off of the [[Object 62|Evil Eye]], also known as a "Nazar", from Middle Eastern mythologies. However, these do not protect one from danger, but simply tell the user that there is danger. These "[[Object 62|Evil Eyes]]", take on the appearance of a small panel, showing a radar, which shows that danger is nearby, and where it is. This "danger", can range as anything from entities, to unstable regions of a level, where accidental no-clipping is likely, to a change in layout, being useful in non-euclidean environments, to even dangerous events, such as ''The Alarms,'' and the floods in [[Level 11.11]]. It does not show what the danger is, however it does tell us that it is there. Instead, it tells us the severity of the supposed danger. A yellow signal signalizes a minor threat, such as entities with threat levels below 3, and dead-ends/possibly dangerous new areas caused by non-euclidean movement. An orange signal signalizes a possible death if not treated with care, such as entities above a threat level of 3, and seriously dangerous environmental hazards, and unstable regions of levels that may lead to the void. A red signal signalizes something that is most definitely going to end in certain death if not taken with an immediate solution. Examples include entities at or, if possible, above a threat level of 5, exits from levels that lead to far worse and more dangerous levels, and dangerous occurrences such as the floods on [[Level 11.11]], and the blackouts on [[Level 1]]. These are also powered by [[Object 61|Everbulbs]], and there are attachments for them located at the back of the device. ===Corrosion Repellent=== [[Object 60|Corrosion Repellent]] is a substance, often found in spray can, and found on the pipes of this level and across some industrial levels, but notably this one. When applied onto rusted metal, it foams up and becomes a deep violet. Once wiped or rinsed off, the rust from the metal is gone, and the metal underneath is restored to perfect conditions. The substance can also de-rust metals up to 5 inches thick. It also removes tarnish and has proven to reverse a few other chemical reactions, the most notable being to unbake bread. This substance is completely safe to ingest, however it isn't particularly recommended as it provides absolutely no nutrition, and can actually be traded for a good amount of supplies. ===Blueprints=== The aforementioned blueprints are found all across the level, and describe mechanisms and recipes that range from no-clip anchors, using the work of [[Object 62|Evil Eyes]] and a lot of other scraps, whose purpose and ability to turn the [[Object 62|Evil Eye]] into a no-clip anchor are unknown. The most seen among the designs are designs of a "Utility Everlight", using an [[Object 61|Everbulb]] to emit the light, using that power source to do things like focus the light into beams that are hot enough to melt lead, focus the light into cones of light, make flashes of light so bright that it may blind wanderers and some entities, assuming they have eyes to see from. ==Entities== Many entities can be found all throughout the level. These can include [[Entity 4|Deathmoths]], [[Entity 3|Smilers]], [[Entity 6|Dullers]], [[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]], [[Entity 8|Hounds]], [[Entity 5|Clumps]], [[Entity 44|Silver Slimes]], and also the level-exclusive Welded Ones and Flesh Dumpsters. ===Flesh Dumpsters=== Flesh Dumpsters inhabit large crevasses. They seem to camouflage themselves perfectly into the metal scraps below. They seem to have a fleshy body underneath the plethora of metal plating to make their disguise. Once falling into one of these crevasses, these entities depend slightly on the debilitation of a limb or major body part in order to be able to successfully capture prey. This means, once you fall into one of these ravines, you will most likely break a limb, making it harder to escape. From there, the entities pick out the mangled bodies and consume them. The exact shape of the entities are unknown, as their camouflage and difficulty to photograph makes it hard to determine. ===Welded Ones/Ironlung Disease "Ferrumasthenes Automatametallosis"=== Ironlung Disease is a highly aggressive viral infection contracted through direct contact or ingestion of contaminated water or sludge found in Level 3.53. [[M.E.G.]] researchers have attempted to research, contain, and cure the disease, but any attempted treatment applied after the victim reached stage 2 were futile. As of now, there are only a few ways to cure it, and action must be taken swiftly in order for such treatments to be effective. It is commonly known as "Ironlung Disease", but the scientific name for this virus is "Ferrumasthenes Automatametallosis". There is a treatment that is only effective before symptoms show, being the only sure-fire cure for this illness. Consuming [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] right after exposure to contaminated waters will stop the illness before it has a chance to occur. =====Stage 1 (1-3 Days after exposure)===== Stage 1 occurs about 1-3 days after initial exposure to any contaminated liquids within the level. Symptoms often include fevers, cold sweats, and tough, gray patches of skin. If these symptoms are noticed, it is highly recommended to seek immediate medical attention, as this is the last point in time in which treatments can take effect. Treatments at this stage include applying [[Object 41|Hydricpoxoic Oil]] or [[Object 60|Corrosion Repellent]] on the affected areas of skin. [[Object 10|Backshrooms]] are deemed ineffective at this stage of illness. These treatments do not guarantee a certain cure. =====Stage 2 – Conversion Process (7-10 Days after initial symptoms)===== Stage 2 marks the irreversible point of the disease. Many makeshift treatments have been tested by [[M.E.G.]], including [[Object 1|Almond Water]], [[Object 10|Backshrooms]], [[Object 41|Hydricpoxoic Oil]], [[Object 60|Corrosion Repellent]], and other makeshift cures and anti-virals along with emergency surgical procedures, all of which have failed to slow progression of this illness after the victim entered the second stage. Symptoms include but are not limited to: *Fevers *Dry coughs, sometimes expelling flakes of metal. *Aches and cramps all across the victims body *The tough, gray patches of skin will begin to harden like sheet metal. *Increased weight in the form of bone mass (caused by conversion of organic materials into metal) *Headaches and impaired vision *Nausea and vomiting =====Stage 3 - Mechanization Process (14+ Days after initial symptoms)===== Stage 3 is the most horrifying point of this illness by far. The first sign of stage 3's oncoming is the absence of any and all pain. Physical features remain, however the victim will report being more alive and energetic for about a day before the reveal begin. At some point, to the horror of the victim and those observing, the flesh of the victim will begin to smoothly fall off, revealing not bones and flesh, but intricate metal mechanisms, that retain their original body part's function. This eventually goes on until there is nothing left of the victim but their brain, which eventually decays as well in about a day after full decomposition and conversion of the body. In rare cases, the victim has reported to not have an absence of pain, and instead of the flesh falling off to reveal intricate, seemingly expertly crafted mechanisms, they reveal nothing but a mess of metal scraps. This results in an immediate and horrifying death for the victim. =====Welded Ones===== The byproduct of this virus ends up becoming a humanoid figure made of intricate metal mechanisms, commonly known by wanderers of this level as the "Welded Ones". They boast immense strength, intelligence, and agility. They have been observed being able to punch their way through concrete, shatter bones with ease, and bend metal for their needs. They also hunt in groups of 2 or 3. They have often been observed making themselves extra limbs with functions such as picks to help them climb all throughout the crevasses located in the level, and to help survive during ''The Alarms''. They seem to only hunt for their needs, but their need for human flesh and organic material is currently unknown. These entities also have reportedly been seen to have the ability to no-clip, mostly on accident. This poses a large threat for [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]] as a whole, as the intelligence boasted by these entities could bring harm to other level's ecosystems. If these entities are spotted anywhere, it is recommended to seek help from any group or person immediately, as their only major and exploitable weakness is that their metal dissolves from certain acids like Hydrochloric and Sulfuric acids. ==Colonies and Outposts== Although this level is extremely difficult to settle in, there is one group that thrives from this level, with their headquarters located here, however they are present in many other levels as well. ===Atlantis Aedificata - The Scrap Baron/S.A.R.I.B.=== The Scrap Barons are a group that collects and trades the very rare and valuable materials and objects in this level for other goods. They have thrived well in this level, and can commonly be found searching piles of rubble for materials, and rope climbing with groups, down into the crevasses located between some levels. *Their headquarters are the located in a hole that they mined out in the Water Reservoirs, this base is known as Atlantis Aedificata. * They are open for trade, and often have very valuable items. * They are accepting new members * They are very friendly, and often have extra room for housing wanderers that come and go. * They mostly consist of seasoned veterans of [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], and are often very capable in leading those in need to other levels. *Their current member count is about 930 across many levels, with about 309 currently in Level 3.53. *In their time in [[Backrooms Wiki|The Backrooms]], they have actually developed a slightly different dialect of modern English. These members are also very crafty and skilled, with some developing suits made by killing Welded Ones, creating utility suits, also including things like the Utility Everlight and a classified piece of technology that apparently allows them to prevent them from accidentally no-clipping to where they wouldn't want to. They have multiple divisions, with the Silverback Division always having atleast a group of 9 in Level 3.53 at all times. ==Extra Information== [[M.E.G.]] expedition groups have managed to recover the following excerpt about Level 3.53 from a half-soaked notebook found in [[Level 37]]. Writer is presumed to be deceased by a [[Entity 3|Smiler]] on [[Level 2]].
Recovered Excerpt: ===Notice - One or more parts of this page were unreadable, seemingly being smudged by ink. === I don’t know how long I’ve been here. The Alarms keep going at random, the hallways keep shifting, the rooms, the floors, it never stays the same. I don't even know how I'm alive right now. It's like every other day that I almost die to a ''welded one'', or fall into the void because of those '''GOD FORSAKEN''' alarms. There’s something in the cracks. I heard it move. It wasn’t just metal shifting—it was breathing. Heavy. Slow. Like it knew I was there. I didn’t stick around to see what it was, obviously, but I keep hearing the scraping now. Like something dragging metal against metal. My arm is getting stiff. Feels colder than the rest of me. Patches of my skin are going gray. I cut my hand on one of the pipes the other day, but it didn't even bleed. It was like there was something under my skin. I need to get out of here. I need to get out '''before I stop being me.''' If you find this, and you still have your body, '''RUN.''' (The rest of this page was smudged in ink.)
==Entrances and Exits== Most of these entrances and exits are very rare or dangerous to execute properly. ===Entrances === *Finding an abandoned factory door in [[Level 3]] may lead to this level. *Crawling through a gap in the pipes in the boiler rooms in [[Level 5]] may eventually lead into a boiler room here. ===Exits=== * Some boiler rooms in this level have staircases that lead up into an abandoned building in the cities of [[Level 41]]. *Very, very rarely will crevasses in the level not have anything below, and instead will lead to [[Level 8]]. *Managing to break out of the safety bunkers after an alarm is over will lead you to [[The Void]]. *During the alarms, while the environment is shifting, you will be able to fall into [[The Void]].
